message:
style(fcp-core): apply rustfmt across nineteen pinned-test files

Nineteen test files in crates/fcp-core/tests/ had drifted from the
project's rustfmt configuration. Most were authored under bead-driven
test-pin tasks where the focus is on what to assert rather than how
the file should be formatted, so they accumulated patterns that
rustfmt re-flows: long panic! / format! macro calls where rustfmt
prefers a wrapped layout, tuple constructors and array literals
where rustfmt prefers each element on its own line once the line
width is exceeded, multi-line panic blocks where rustfmt prefers a
brace-and-block form, and array iteration cases where the inline
form is past the width budget.

Running rustfmt across them brings the suite back into a consistent
canonical form so that future agent diffs are signal (semantic test
changes) rather than noise (formatting churn that shows up alongside
real edits). Same intent as the earlier sweep in commit e85d8b7d9.

Each diff in this commit is mechanically reproducible: re-running
cargo fmt against any of these files produces no further output.
Zero assertions added or removed, zero fixture values changed,
zero new tests, zero new imports beyond what rustfmt re-orders.

Files touched (all pure rustfmt output):
- capability_revocation_display.rs
- connector_activation_stage_display.rs
- connector_bundle_serde_extended.rs
- connector_bundle_size_serde.rs
- connector_class_serde_tag.rs
- connector_context_serde.rs
- connector_entitlement_serde.rs
- connector_policy_decision_serde_matrix.rs
- connector_topology_serde.rs
- enforcement_group_variant_display.rs
- enforcement_outcome_roundtrip.rs
- lease_grant_display_serde.rs
- manifest_policy_variant_matrix.rs
- manifest_status_variant_matrix.rs
- registry_query_serde_roundtrip.rs
- retention_policy_variant_display.rs
- signed_attestation_roundtrip.rs
- signer_kind_serde_tag_matrix.rs
- zone_event_serde_tag_matrix.rs

Typical patterns:
- panic!(...) that exceeds line-width gets wrapped into brace-block.
- Tuple-of-variants arrays: each tuple on its own multi-line form.
- assert_eq!(a, b, msg) collapsed/wrapped per width budget.
- for bad in ["LwwMap", "OrSet", ...] reformatted one-per-line
  when inline form exceeds width.

There is no path through these files where behaviour can change
as a result of rustfmt reflowing whitespace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
